topics.title: Europe's heatwave breaks more records
topics.summary: Europe's June heatwave kept intensifying, with Germany and Denmark reporting record temperatures, travel disruption and growing concerns about farms, rivers and glaciers.
topics.discussionPrompt: When summer heat starts affecting roads, trains and rivers, does it make you rethink what a normal vacation season should look like?

topics.title: World Cup knockout picture takes shape
topics.summary: The expanded World Cup is moving into its round of 32, with co-hosts Canada, Mexico and the United States advancing and several high-profile matchups now set.
topics.discussionPrompt: With more teams in the tournament, do you like the bigger World Cup format, or does it make the whole thing feel a bit crowded?

topics.title: Cape Verde extends its World Cup dream
topics.summary: Cape Verde reached the World Cup knockout stage in its tournament debut, becoming one of the standout underdog stories before a round-of-32 meeting with Argentina.
topics.discussionPrompt: Sports are always more fun with an underdog. Do you root for the surprise team, or stick with the established favourites?

topics.title: Swedish startups push back on immigration rules
topics.summary: Swedish business leaders warned that tighter immigration policies could hurt startups by making it harder to attract and keep international talent.
topics.discussionPrompt: For fast-moving tech companies, how important is it to bring in talent from anywhere, even when voters want tighter immigration rules?
